project.name: Legatura
project.description: A trusted change framework for agentic software engineering.

fact.owns: Canonical digests, process observations, and exact Git bindings.
Project Model and Development Plan interpretation, validation, Context Capsules, impact, and verification planning.
Change lifecycle, Evidence coverage, acceptance, integration assurance, and package integrity.
CLI, loopback HTTP interface, browser projection, and package delivery behavior.

governed.reason: Relationships, scope compilation, and Claim mappings are executable and covered by invariant tests.
Lifecycle, Evidence, authority, tamper resistance, and integration transitions have adversarial tests.

provisional.reason: Git exact binding and the local-direct-node-posix-v1 process profile now have bounded fail-closed proof for raw streams, encoding validity, exit, signal, timeout, cancellation, and settlement; the Module remains provisional because Windows, shell, other Node majors, escaped descendants, remote workers, and cross-host conformance are explicitly unsupported or outside this profile.
CLI and HTTP behavior are tested, but the browser UI lacks DOM-level end-to-end verification.

opaque.reason: Worker spawning and filesystem capability enforcement do not exist yet.
Local decidedBy values are not authenticated or signed.
